questions.question: Explain the concept of exception chaining in Java with an example.
keyPoints: Exception chaining links one exception to another
Allows preserving original exception information while throwing new exception
Used to add contextual information while maintaining root cause
Involves initCause() or constructor with cause parameter

questions.question: What is the purpose of initCause() and getCause() methods in exception handling?
keyPoints: initCause() sets the cause of an exception
getCause() retrieves the original exception that was chained
initCause() returns the exception for method chaining
getCause() returns null if no cause was set

questions.question: How does exception chaining help in debugging multi-layered applications?
keyPoints: Preserves complete stack trace from all layers
Allows tracing back to root cause
Each layer can add contextual information
Helps identify where the actual error originated

questions.question: Write a Java code snippet demonstrating exception chaining using constructor approach.
keyPoints: Catch the original exception
Create new exception with message and cause using constructor
Throw the new exception
Print stack trace to show complete chain

questions.question: What is the difference between chained exceptions and nested try-catch blocks?
keyPoints: Chained exceptions link exceptions causally using initCause/getCause
Nested try-catch is structural nesting of try blocks
Chained exceptions form a cause-effect relationship
Nested try-catch handles exceptions at different levels of code

questions.question: Why is it recommended to avoid exposing low-level exceptions to API users?
keyPoints: Low-level exceptions expose implementation details
They may not be meaningful to API consumers
Chaining allows throwing meaningful high-level exceptions
Preserves encapsulation and abstraction

questions.question: How can you traverse a complete exception chain to find the root cause?
keyPoints: Use getCause() method repeatedly
Iterate until getCause() returns null
The last exception with null cause is the root cause
Can use while loop to traverse entire chain

questions.question: Write a complete Java program demonstrating exception chaining in a multi-layered application scenario (e.g., DAO, Service, Controller layers). Include proper exception handling at each layer and show how the root cause is preserved.
keyPoints: Create custom exceptions for each layer
Implement method that catches lower-layer exception and throws new with cause
Demonstrate propagation through layers
Show output of printStackTrace displaying complete chain
Use both initCause() and constructor approaches
Explain how root cause is preserved

questions.question: Explain with code examples the various ways to implement exception chaining in Java. Compare the constructor approach versus initCause() method approach.
keyPoints: Show constructor approach: new Exception(msg, cause)
Show initCause approach: exception.initCause(original)
Compare advantages of each
Show method chaining with initCause return value
Mention limitation of initCause being callable only once
Provide complete working code examples

questions.question: Design a custom exception hierarchy for a banking application that uses exception chaining. Show how exceptions like InsufficientFundsException, TransactionFailedException, and AccountNotFoundException would be chained when a transaction fails at the database level.
keyPoints: Create custom exception classes extending appropriate base
Design the chain: DBException -> AccountNotFoundException -> InsufficientFundsException -> TransactionFailedException
Implement proper constructors with cause parameter
Show practical usage in banking operations
Demonstrate how printStackTrace reveals full chain
Discuss best practices in exception design

questions.question: Write a Java method that demonstrates best practices for exception chaining, including: checking for existing cause, handling IllegalStateException, and properly documenting exceptions.
keyPoints: Check if cause already exists using getCause()
Use appropriate constructors
Handle case where initCause may fail
Document thrown exceptions with Javadoc
Show proper logging of complete chain
Include exception chaining anti-patterns to avoid
